items.title: Plugin metadata snapshots are shallow and lose registered metadata during registry cleanup
items.recommendation: Copy metadata with the same per-plugin-type pattern used for plugins, for example `{pt: dict(d) for pt, d in PluginRegistry._metadata.items()}`, in both fixtures before clearing the registry.
items.reproduction: Register any plugin with metadata, run a test using clear_registry or a class that clears PluginRegistry, then check PluginRegistry.get_plugin_metadata for the originally registered plugin after teardown; it returns {} because the saved inner metadata dict was cleared in place.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included tests exercise auto-config and alias behavior, but none assert that PluginRegistry metadata survives the plugin test fixtures' teardown path.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a small plugin conftest regression test that seeds PluginRegistry._metadata with a per-type entry, exercises clear_registry, and verifies the metadata entry is restored after fixture teardown.
items.minimumFixScope: Update the two metadata snapshot assignments in tests/core/plugins/conftest.py to deep-copy the per-type metadata dictionaries.

items.title: Negative max_rows renders nearly the whole table instead of rejecting the limit
items.recommendation: Add validation constraints such as Field(default=100, ge=1) for max_rows in both parameter models, or explicitly guard in render and return a configuration error when max_rows is less than 1.
items.reproduction: Pass RawDataWidgetParams(max_rows=-1) or TableViewParams(max_rows=-1) with a 100-row DataFrame. The widget renders 99 rows rather than rejecting the configuration or rendering no rows.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: RawDataWidget tests only assert that invalid values are accepted by the model and do not render with them; TableView tests cover positive limits only.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add render tests for max_rows=0 and max_rows=-1 on RawDataWidget and TableViewWidget that expect a configuration error, then update parameter validation tests to expect a validation error for values below 1.
items.minimumFixScope: RawDataWidgetParams and TableViewParams max_rows validation, with tests in test_raw_data_widget.py and test_table_view.py.

items.title: Une hiérarchie avec cardinalités égales est validée comme forte
items.recommendation: Rejeter aussi les égalités en remplaçant la condition par `>=`, ou définir explicitement une tolérance documentée si les égalités doivent être acceptées dans certains cas.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Les tests trouvés pour ce fichier ciblent la détection de relations et `analyze_file_columns`; aucun test n’exerce `_validate_hierarchy` avec des cardinalités égales.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Appeler `detect_hierarchy_columns` avec deux niveaux connus et un échantillon où les deux colonnes ont exactement le même nombre de valeurs uniques, puis vérifier que `is_valid` est faux ou que la confiance est faible selon le contrat retenu.
items.minimumFixScope: Ajuster la condition de validation dans `_validate_hierarchy` et ajouter un test unitaire couvrant l’égalité de cardinalité.

items.title: Default entity inference order depends on set iteration
items.recommendation: Define ENTITY_SYNONYMS from an explicit ordered tuple of entity names, or make infer_entity_token use a stable default priority such as ("plot", "taxon", "locality", "occurrence", "observation").
items.reproduction: Run infer_entity_token("site_species") under different PYTHONHASHSEED values; because it can match both locality and taxon, the result follows the arbitrary ENTITY_SYNONYMS insertion order.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The domain vocabulary tests pass an explicit allowed tuple, so they avoid the unordered default candidate path.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a test for infer_entity_token without allowed using an ambiguous value and assert the intended stable priority.
items.minimumFixScope: Change ENTITY_SYNONYMS construction or infer_entity_token default ordering in src/niamoto/core/domain_vocabulary.py, with one regression test in tests/core/test_domain_vocabulary.py.

items.title: Image URLs with query strings are misclassified as plain links
items.recommendation: Parse URLs before extension matching, or strip query strings and fragments before checking _IMAGE_URL_EXTENSIONS. Keep the existing keyword-based fallback for extensionless media URLs.
items.reproduction: Call build_enrichment_catalog with a source data value like {"image_url": "https://img.example/photo.jpg?width=800"}; the resulting field format is "link" rather than "image".
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included enrichment display tests only use image URLs ending directly in .jpg, so they do not exercise common CDN/query-string image URLs.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a test asserting that an image URL with a query string, for example https://img.example/flower.jpg?width=800, produces format "image" and appears in the media section.
items.minimumFixScope: Update _looks_like_image_url in src/niamoto/core/enrichment_display.py and add one focused test in tests/core/test_enrichment_display.py.

items.title: Highest elevation bin excludes pixels equal to the final edge
items.recommendation: For the last bin, use <= bin_edges[i + 1] while keeping < for earlier bins, or delegate bin assignment to np.histogram/np.digitize with explicit right-edge handling so all distributions share one binning rule.
items.reproduction: Use custom bins [0, 100, 200] and elevation data containing a forest pixel with value 200. The elevation histogram counts it in the 100-200 class, but _calculate_forest_distribution and ForestElevationAnalysis do not include it in any bin.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The elevation tests use data whose maximum value is below the final edge, such as 990 with a 1000 final edge, so the right-edge case is never exercised.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add an elevation overlay test and a forest_elevation test where one valid forest pixel equals the final bin edge, then assert the final class includes that pixel.
items.minimumFixScope: Update the bin-mask construction in ElevationProfile._calculate_forest_distribution and ForestElevationAnalysis.transform to include the upper edge only for the final bin.

items.title: GeospatialExtractor advertises dict input but crashes before selecting a source DataFrame
items.recommendation: Normalize input at the start of transform: if data is a dict, select data[source] when present, otherwise fetch from the configured source or use an empty DataFrame. Only access .empty and .columns after data is guaranteed to be a DataFrame.
items.reproduction: Call GeospatialExtractor.transform({"occurrences": pd.DataFrame({"geometry": [Point(1, 2)]})}, {"plugin":"geospatial_extractor","params":{"source":"occurrences","field":"geometry"}}). It reaches `if field not in data.columns` and fails because dict has no `columns`. For source "plots", it can fail earlier at `data.empty`.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Geospatial tests cover DataFrame input and external source loading, but none pass a dict of source DataFrames despite the public type signature and sibling transformer behavior.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a geospatial transform test with data={"occurrences": dataframe_with_geometry} and another with data={"plots": dataframe_with_geometry}, asserting both return valid FeatureCollections without database loading.
items.minimumFixScope: Update GeospatialExtractor.transform input normalization and add dict-input tests.

items.title: Research scripts have no regression tests for command orchestration or cache safety
items.recommendation: Add focused unit tests around pure and boundary-heavy helpers: argument validation and subprocess command construction in `ablation_run.py`, cache manifest/replacement behavior in `build_fusion_surrogate_cache.py`, and scope rejection/restoration paths in `run_fusion_surrogate_autoresearch.py` with subprocess and git calls mocked.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The feature declares an empty `tests` list, so there is no included test evidence covering intended behavior for these scripts.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Create a `tests/ml/scripts/research/` test module that monkeypatches subprocess/git/evaluation calls and covers success, command failure, forbidden-file rejection, and cache replacement rollback.
items.minimumFixScope: Add targeted tests without changing production behavior, prioritizing the two concrete failure modes above.

items.title: Root transformer exports omit registered transformer classes
items.recommendation: Either update the root package imports and __all__ to include every transformer that is intended to be publicly available from niamoto.core.plugins.transformers, or narrow the comment/API contract so this module is explicitly only a curated compatibility export list.
items.reproduction: Attempting `from niamoto.core.plugins.transformers import NiamotoHumboldtEventTransformer` will fail because the root package imports only NiamotoDwCTransformer from the formats package and __all__ only exposes NiamotoToDwcOccurrenceTransformer for format conversion.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The package export test only asserts that TransformChain is included and that existing __all__ names are bound; it does not assert that all registered transformer modules are represented in the root export surface.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a test that compares the intended root exports against registered transformer classes, or at least asserts root imports for omitted public transformers such as NiamotoHumboldtEventTransformer and MultiColumnExtractor.
items.minimumFixScope: src/niamoto/core/plugins/transformers/__init__.py and the package export contract test.

items.title: Schema-less chain steps cannot be referenced by later validated steps
items.recommendation: Register the output_key for schema-less plugins as an available step with unknown fields, then either skip field-level validation for references to unknown-schema outputs or emit a warning instead of an unknown-step error.
items.reproduction: Call TransformChainValidator.validate_chain with step 1 using a registered custom transformer not present in PLUGIN_SCHEMAS and output_key="custom", followed by a binned_distribution step whose params include a reference such as @custom.value. The validator warns for step 1, then raises "references unknown step 'custom'" for step 2.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The provided tests exercise TransformChain execution and ReferenceResolver behavior, but there are no tests for TransformChainValidator or chains that reference outputs from plugins without static IO schemas.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a chain_validator test where a registered schema-less plugin is followed by a known-schema plugin that references its output; assert validation returns a warning but does not report the step as unknown.
items.minimumFixScope: Adjust TransformChainValidator.validate_chain so schema-less steps still populate available_outputs, using a sentinel for unknown fields.

items.title: Leaflet renderer ignores custom tile configuration
items.recommendation: Serialize the configured tile URL and attribution into the Leaflet template, falling back to the existing CARTO values only when no custom tile URL is provided. Escape or JSON-encode both values before embedding them in JavaScript.
items.reproduction: Call MapRenderer.render with engine='leaflet' and MapConfig(custom_tiles_url='/tiles/{z}/{x}/{y}.png', custom_tiles_attribution='Local tiles'); the returned HTML still contains the CARTO URL and attribution and never references the configured values.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests were provided for custom tile handling across rendering engines.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a Leaflet rendering test that passes a custom_tiles_url and custom_tiles_attribution and asserts both appear in the generated HTML while the default CARTO URL does not.
items.minimumFixScope: Update MapRenderer._render_leaflet to consume MapConfig.custom_tiles_url and custom_tiles_attribution.

items.title: Valid but non-object config JSON is silently discarded
items.recommendation: After decoding JSON, require the result to be a Mapping before constructing metadata. If it is not a mapping, raise DatabaseQueryError with the relevant lookup query and config details. Apply the same validation to both registries.
items.reproduction: Insert a row into niamoto_metadata_transform_sources with config='[]' or config='null', then call TransformSourceRegistry.get(name). The registry should reject the invalid config shape but can return metadata with an empty or invalid config instead of a DatabaseQueryError.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included source-registry test only covers syntactically invalid JSON. It does not cover syntactically valid JSON with the wrong shape, and there is no included EntityRegistry test covering corrupt config payloads.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add parametrized tests that insert config values like '[]', 'null', and '"bad"' into each metadata table and assert get()/list_* raise DatabaseQueryError instead of returning empty or invalid config data.
items.minimumFixScope: Validate decoded payload type in EntityRegistry._row_to_metadata and TransformSourceRegistry._row_to_metadata, plus focused tests for valid non-object JSON payloads.

items.title: Uppercase CSV paths saved by the API are omitted from the sources list
items.recommendation: Normalize the filter to Path(data_path).suffix.lower() == '.csv' or data_path.lower().endswith('.csv') so it matches the validator's accepted contract.
items.reproduction: Save a source whose file_path is an imports-relative file ending in .CSV, then call GET /api/sources/{reference_name}/sources. The saved source is skipped by the route's case-sensitive filter.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The existing canonical path test saves imports/raw_taxa_stats.csv with a lowercase suffix, and there is no GET /sources test that verifies saved CSV sources are returned.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a test that writes imports/raw_taxa_stats.CSV, saves it through POST /save, then asserts GET /sources includes the configured source.
items.minimumFixScope: Change the CSV filter in get_group_sources to be case-insensitive.

items.title: GET /config lacks direct route coverage
items.recommendation: Add focused TestClient coverage for /api/export/config using a temporary project config, including at least a list-form exports config and the expected summary/config/raw_config response.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The associated tests patch and call export execution/history paths, but no included test sends GET /api/export/config or asserts get_export_config_endpoint output.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Create a temporary NIAMOTO_HOME/config/export.yml with two exports, call GET /api/export/config, and assert status 200, summary.total_exports, summary.export_types counts, normalized config.exports keys, and raw_config preservation.
items.minimumFixScope: Add route-level tests for get_export_config_endpoint; no production code change is required unless the new tests expose a contract mismatch.

items.title: GET /jobs route lacks direct API test coverage
items.recommendation: Add focused FastAPI tests for `/jobs` using a mocked or injected `JobFileStore`, covering active job inclusion, history inclusion, deduplication, non-transform filtering, and empty history.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: `tests/cli/test_transform.py` invokes Click commands through `CliRunner`; it does not instantiate the API router, `TestClient`, or `list_transform_jobs`.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a route test that overrides/resolves the app job store, calls `GET /jobs`, and asserts the returned `jobs` list contains only transform jobs with `job_id`, `status`, `started_at`, `completed_at`, `progress`, and `message`.
items.minimumFixScope: Add a dedicated API test module for transform router job listing behavior.

items.title: GET /jobs can return fewer than the recent transform jobs when history contains other job types
items.recommendation: Fetch transform history with a type filter in `JobFileStore` if available, or request enough history and stop after collecting 10 transform jobs. Keep the active-job deduplication after the transform-specific selection.
items.reproduction: Create 1 completed transform job, then create 10 newer non-transform jobs in the shared job history. `GET /jobs` will omit the transform job because it is filtered out after `get_history(limit=10)` has already truncated the history.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The linked tests exercise the CLI transform command, not the FastAPI `/jobs` endpoint or mixed-type job history behavior.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a FastAPI route test with a fake job store containing one transform job older than 10 non-transform jobs, then assert `GET /jobs` still returns the transform job when asking for recent transform jobs.
items.minimumFixScope: Change only `list_transform_jobs` and, if needed, add a small typed-history helper to `JobFileStore`.

items.title: GET /spatial leaks unexpected exception details in 500 responses
items.recommendation: Log the exception server-side and return a generic detail such as "Error getting spatial stats" for unexpected failures.
items.reproduction: Monkeypatch a helper used by GET /spatial, for example _resolve_entity_table or _compute_geometry_count_and_bbox, to raise RuntimeError("secret /tmp/spatial.sql"), then call /api/stats/spatial. The response detail will include the secret string.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: tests/cli/test_stats.py covers the Click stats command, not the FastAPI route. tests/gui/api/routers/test_stats.py covers successful /api/stats/spatial responses and sanitization for /spatial-map, but not unexpected-error sanitization for /spatial.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a GUI API route test that monkeypatches get_spatial_stats internals to raise RuntimeError("secret /tmp/spatial.sql"), calls /api/stats/spatial, asserts status 500, asserts detail is the generic message, and asserts the secret text is absent from the response body.
items.minimumFixScope: Update only get_spatial_stats unexpected-exception handling and add the focused route regression test.

items.title: CSV import accepts blank normalized headers and returns rows with empty keys
items.recommendation: Reject blank normalized headers with a 400 response before building entries, or assign explicit fallback column names only when has_header=false.
items.reproduction: POST /api/site/import-csv with file contents `,name\nfoo,bar\n` returns success with data like `[{"":"foo","name":"bar"}]`.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The CSV tests cover duplicate normalized headers, invalid delimiters, a valid semicolon import, and oversized uploads, but none sends a CSV whose header row contains an empty cell.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a test posting `,name\nfoo,bar\n` to /api/site/import-csv and assert a 400 response such as `CSV headers must not be blank`.
items.minimumFixScope: Update import_csv header validation and add one route-level regression test.

items.title: POST selector validation allows ambiguous template_id and inline payloads
items.recommendation: Change validation to require exactly one selector, unless the intended contract is that inline overrides template_id. If both are intentionally supported, document the precedence and add a test that locks it down.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The tests cover inline-only, template_id-only, and empty-body rejection, but not the mixed selector case.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: POST /api/preview with both template_id and inline should return 422 and assert engine.render is not called, or should assert the documented precedence if both are intentionally accepted.
items.minimumFixScope: Update InlinePreviewBody.require_preview_selector and the associated POST route tests for the both-provided case.

items.title: Invalid WKT rows are counted as georeferenced when no usable shape table exists
items.recommendation: In the no_shapes branch, count valid geometries using the same normalization as the success path, preferably by calling _count_valid_geometries or by using _geometry_sql_expression with a spatial-extension fallback consistent with get_geo_coverage.
items.reproduction: Create occurrences with geo_pt values ['POINT (166.5 -21.5)', 'not a geometry', NULL] and no spatial reference table with geometry, then POST /api/stats/geo-coverage/analyze. The no_shapes response will report occurrences_with_geo=2 instead of 1.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: tests/cli/test_stats.py exercises the CLI stats command, not this FastAPI route. The GUI regression test covers invalid WKT only when a shape table with geometry exists, so execution goes through the success branch rather than the no_shapes branch.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a FastAPI route test where occurrences contain one valid WKT, one invalid WKT, and one NULL geometry, with no usable spatial shape table; assert status == 'no_shapes', occurrences_with_geo == 1, and occurrences_without_geo == 2.
items.minimumFixScope: Update analyze_spatial_coverage's no_shapes branch to use the same valid-geometry counting semantics as the main analysis path, plus one route regression test for invalid WKT without shapes.

items.title: User-provided credentials and worker URL are silently ignored
items.recommendation: Remove worker_url and api_key from the route signature if this endpoint is intentionally configuration-only, or explicitly support and validate them if per-request overrides are part of the public contract. Given the tests, removal is the smaller and safer fix.
items.reproduction: Call POST /api/feedback/submit with worker_url and api_key form fields different from the configured environment values; the request succeeds using the configured values and ignores the submitted fields.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The current test covers the intended security behavior by confirming submitted worker_url/api_key are ignored, but it does not catch the API contract problem that these unused fields remain exposed in the endpoint schema.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Assert that the generated OpenAPI schema for POST /api/feedback/submit only documents the supported multipart fields, excluding worker_url and api_key when they are not meant to be accepted.
items.minimumFixScope: Update submit_feedback's FastAPI signature and adjust any tests or OpenAPI expectations that reference the unused form fields.

items.title: Job status response can leak the local working directory
items.recommendation: Either require the same desktop read/auth protection for job status responses, or omit/redact local-only fields such as working_directory before serializing job status.
items.reproduction: Create an import-all job with working_directory set to an absolute path, then request GET /api/imports/jobs/{job_id}; the JSON response includes the working_directory value.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The current get_job_status test only asserts traceback redaction and uses a base job whose working_directory is None, so it would not catch absolute path disclosure.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a GET /api/imports/jobs/{job_id} test with working_directory set to a private absolute path and assert the response omits or redacts that field.
items.minimumFixScope: Update _sanitize_import_job_for_response or get_job_status to remove/redact working_directory, and add a focused API test for that response contract.

items.title: Z-score responses always omit the computed standard deviation
items.recommendation: Track the computed standard deviation in a local variable and pass it to ColumnValidation, at least for the zscore path. If std_dev is intended to be method-independent, compute it alongside min/max/avg instead.
items.reproduction: Create a numeric table with multiple distinct non-null values, call GET /api/stats/value-validation/<table>?columns=<numeric_column>&method=zscore, and observe lower_bound/upper_bound are populated while std_dev remains null.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included CLI stats tests do not exercise this FastAPI route. The existing GUI route tests cover histogram boundaries, missing/non-numeric columns, and zero median preservation, but they do not assert std_dev for zscore responses.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a GUI API test that creates a table with values such as 1, 2, and 100, requests method=zscore, and asserts payload['columns'][0]['std_dev'] is non-null and positive.
items.minimumFixScope: src/niamoto/gui/api/routers/stats.py get_value_validation zscore response assembly, plus one focused GUI API regression test.

items.title: Unexpected validation failures are returned to network clients verbatim
items.recommendation: Log the full exception server-side and return a generic 500 detail such as 'Error validating values'. Keep explicit HTTPException branches for expected 400/404/422 validation errors.
items.reproduction: In a route test, monkeypatch a dependency used by get_value_validation, such as open_database or db.get_columns, to raise RuntimeError('secret /tmp/internal.sql'), then request /api/stats/value-validation/<entity>; the response detail includes the secret string.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included CLI stats tests do not cover GUI API error responses. Existing GUI tests include this security expectation for spatial-map inspection, but no equivalent test covers value-validation unexpected failures.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a GUI API test that monkeypatches value-validation internals to raise an exception containing a sentinel secret and asserts the 500 response does not include that sentinel.
items.minimumFixScope: src/niamoto/gui/api/routers/stats.py get_value_validation catch-all error handler, plus one focused GUI API regression test.

items.title: Column metadata endpoint exposes internal SQLite tables hidden from table listing
items.recommendation: Apply the same visibility predicate used by list_tables before calling db.get_columns, for example reject table_name.startswith("sqlite_") with the same 404 response used for missing tables. Consider extracting a shared helper for visible data-explorer tables so list, query, and columns remain consistent.
items.reproduction: Against a SQLite-backed project containing an autoincrement table, request GET /api/data/tables/sqlite_sequence/columns. The endpoint will pass the membership check if sqlite_sequence is in db.get_table_names() and return its columns instead of treating it as unavailable.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included route tests cover a normal visible table and a missing table, but they do not create or request an internal sqlite_ table. The list_tables test asserts visible fixture tables only, so it does not catch that direct column lookup bypasses the listing filter.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a SQLite-backed API test that creates an autoincrement table, asserts sqlite_sequence is not present in GET /api/data/tables, then asserts GET /api/data/tables/sqlite_sequence/columns returns 404.
items.minimumFixScope: src/niamoto/gui/api/routers/data_explorer.py plus a focused test in tests/gui/api/routers/test_data_explorer.py

items.title: Reference discovery opens the DuckDB database once per reference
items.recommendation: Reuse a single read-only Database connection for both counts and hierarchy column inspection, collecting table columns for each reference before closing the connection once. If DB metadata lookup fails, consider logging the exception while still returning the config-derived references.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: There are no /references route tests, and the included Config tests do not create a DuckDB file or assert how many times Database is opened.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Mock config_router.Database and call /api/config/references with multiple references plus an existing db path; assert the route opens Database once while returning counts and hierarchy metadata for each reference.
items.minimumFixScope: Refactor the DB enrichment portion of get_references to share the existing connection across registry, count, and column detection work.

items.title: GET /references has no direct route coverage
items.recommendation: Add focused TestClient tests for /api/config/references covering at least: missing import.yml returns an empty response, a valid references section returns normalized schema_fields and total, and malformed/non-dict reference entries are handled intentionally.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: tests/common/test_config.py only constructs Config directly and calls unrelated routes; there is no request to /api/config/references or assertion on ReferencesResponse.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Create a temporary project with config/import.yml containing two entities.references entries, patch config_router.get_working_directory to that project, call TestClient(create_app()).get('/api/config/references'), and assert status 200 plus references, total, table_name fallback, schema_fields, and enrichment flags.
items.minimumFixScope: Add route-level tests in tests/common/test_config.py or a dedicated GUI API router test module.

items.title: GET /project route has no direct test coverage
items.recommendation: Add focused TestClient tests for GET /api/config/project covering missing config.yml, a normal project section, optional fields, and malformed-but-valid YAML shapes.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Existing tests in tests/common/test_config.py mostly target the Config class and two transform widget route cases; they do not assert the /project API contract.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add `test_get_project_info_returns_defaults_without_config` and `test_get_project_info_reads_project_section` in the linked test module or a GUI router-specific test file.
items.minimumFixScope: Add tests only; no production change is required for the coverage gap itself.

items.title: Malformed project section turns a read-only route into a 500
items.recommendation: Normalize the loaded YAML before accessing it: verify the root is a dict, verify `project` is a dict when present, and either fall back to default project metadata for non-dict project values or raise an explicit HTTPException with a stable error message.
items.reproduction: Create config/config.yml with `project:` under the selected working directory, then call GET /api/config/project. The handler loads `project` as None and raises `'NoneType' object has no attribute 'get'`, returned as HTTP 500.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The linked tests exercise Config loading and a different FastAPI config route, but none call GET /project with malformed or partial config.yml content.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a TestClient regression that patches get_working_directory to a temporary project containing `config/config.yml` with `project:` and asserts GET /api/config/project returns a controlled non-500 response with the default name/version or a stable 400 detail.
items.minimumFixScope: Update get_project_info in src/niamoto/gui/api/routers/config.py and add a focused route test.

items.title: Delete mutation auth is not covered through the actual FastAPI route
items.recommendation: Add TestClient coverage for DELETE /api/imports/entities/{entity_type}/{entity_name} with NIAMOTO_DESKTOP_AUTH_TOKEN set: one request without x-niamoto-desktop-token should return 401 and preserve import.yml; one request with the token should reach the delete path.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Other import endpoints have auth tests, but the delete route's own tests bypass the ASGI route and auth configuration.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Use TestClient(create_app()) against /api/imports/entities/reference/plots?delete_table=false with and without x-niamoto-desktop-token while asserting config preservation on the rejected request.
items.minimumFixScope: Add route-level auth tests for delete_entity; no production code change is required unless the new test exposes a failure.

items.title: Internal exception messages are returned to API clients
items.recommendation: Keep logger.exception for server diagnostics, but replace the client-facing detail with a stable generic message such as 'Failed to detect semantic groups'.
items.reproduction: Patch Database, EntityRegistry, or entity_meta.config access to raise an exception containing a sensitive path or backend detail, then call GET /api/templates/{reference_name}/semantic-groups; the response detail will include that raw message.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The linked tests cover the successful empty response for a missing registry entity and the 400 response for an invalid cross-reference source, but they do not cover the unexpected-exception path or assert that 500 responses avoid leaking raw exception text.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a route test that forces EntityRegistry or registry.get to raise RuntimeError('/private/project/path failed') and asserts status_code == 500 while response.json()['detail'] is the generic message and does not contain the injected path.
items.minimumFixScope: Change only the broad exception handler in get_semantic_groups to return a generic detail string, plus add the focused regression test.

items.title: SSE stream can skip retained events after job event pruning
items.recommendation: Track a monotonically increasing event sequence number per job and stream events by sequence, or have the store return a snapshot plus the retained base offset so the stream can adjust next_index when pruning occurs. At minimum, clamp next_index after pruning so it is relative to the current retained list.
items.reproduction: Create a store with a low max_events_per_job, open the events stream, append enough events to trigger deletion from the front while the stream has already advanced next_index, then append additional non-terminal events. The subsequent current_job.events[next_index:] slice skips some retained events because next_index still counts from the old list head.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The tests verify that the store prunes to the last two events and that normal/terminal SSE streams deliver complete or error events, but they do not combine an active stream with event pruning during a running job.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add an SSE/unit test using _AutoConfigureJobStore(max_events_per_job=2) or a monkeypatched global store that starts streaming, appends events past the retention limit, then verifies newly retained events are still emitted in order instead of skipped.
items.minimumFixScope: Update the auto-config job event storage and stream cursor logic in src/niamoto/gui/api/routers/smart_config.py, plus a focused regression test in tests/gui/api/routers/test_smart_config.py.

items.title: Directory listings follow child symlinks and can expose metadata outside the project
items.recommendation: When listing directory entries, use lstat()/is_symlink() or os.scandir with follow_symlinks=False. Either omit symlink entries, mark them as symlink without following them, or resolve each child and include metadata only if the resolved child remains under the project root.
items.reproduction: Create a project directory with a symlink child pointing to an external file, monkeypatch get_working_directory to that project, then GET /api/files/browse for the containing directory. The response item for the symlink is reported as a file and includes the external target's size.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included browse test only covers a missing path returning 404. It does not create symlinks inside a browsed directory or assert that child metadata is collected without following symlinks.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a /browse test that creates a symlink inside the project pointing to a file outside the project and asserts the response does not expose the external target's size or type as a normal file.
items.minimumFixScope: Update browse_files directory-entry metadata collection and add a focused symlink listing regression test.

items.title: GET reference config route lacks direct regression coverage
items.recommendation: Add focused FastAPI TestClient coverage for GET /api/config/references/{reference_name}/config covering success, missing reference, and missing import.yml.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The linked tests/common/test_config.py file contains Config model tests and unrelated API smoke coverage, but no request to the reference config endpoint. A search of the inspected linked tests found dataset config route tests, but no equivalent reference config route tests.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Create a temporary project config/import.yml with entities.references.taxonomy, monkeypatch get_working_directory, then assert GET /api/config/references/taxonomy/config returns 200 with {'name': 'taxonomy', 'config': ...}; add 404 assertions for missing reference and missing import.yml.
items.minimumFixScope: Tests only; no production code change is required for this finding.

items.title: Analyze route reports malformed configured CSVs as server errors
items.recommendation: Validate the file before invoking ClassObjectAnalyzer, mirroring upload_precalc_source's empty-file check, and translate known CSV/parsing errors to HTTP 400 while preserving unexpected errors as 500.
items.reproduction: Configure a source in transform.yml whose data points to an empty CSV under imports, then call GET /api/sources/taxons/analyze/<source_name>. ClassObjectAnalyzer will fail while reading the CSV header and the route will return 500 via the broad exception handler.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included analyze test only covers path traversal rejection. Empty CSV handling is tested for save_source_config, but there is no corresponding analyze_existing_source regression test for empty or malformed configured files.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add test_analyze_existing_source_rejects_empty_csv that writes a transform.yml source pointing at an empty imports/*.csv file, calls /api/sources/{reference}/analyze/{source}, and asserts HTTP 400 with a validation-oriented detail instead of 500.
items.minimumFixScope: src/niamoto/gui/api/routers/sources.py analyze_existing_source error handling plus one focused test in tests/gui/api/routers/test_sources.py

items.title: PUT /data-content returns an unhandled server error when the target JSON path is a directory
items.recommendation: Before creating a backup, check `if file_path.exists() and not file_path.is_file(): raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Path is not a file")`. Keep this check after containment and extension validation but before `_create_backup(file_path)`.
items.reproduction: Create a project with `data/items.json/` as a directory, then call `PUT /api/site/data-content` with `{"path":"data/items.json","data":[]}`. The backup step attempts to open the directory and the request fails as a server error rather than returning a client-facing validation response.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included site router tests cover GET rejecting non-array JSON data, but they do not exercise PUT against an existing directory or other non-file path under an allowed data root.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a PUT test that creates `project/data/items.json` as a directory, calls `/api/site/data-content`, and asserts status 400 with `Path is not a file` and no backup/temp files left behind.
items.minimumFixScope: Add the missing existing-path `is_file()` validation in `update_data_content` and cover it with one route-level regression test.

items.title: Runtime mode response can violate its documented enum contract
items.recommendation: Normalize the environment value before returning it, for example return "desktop" only when NIAMOTO_RUNTIME_MODE == "desktop" and "web" otherwise, then derive feature flags from the normalized value.
items.reproduction: Set NIAMOTO_RUNTIME_MODE=invalid and call GET /api/health/runtime-mode; the response contains {"mode":"invalid","features":{"project_switching":false}} instead of normalizing to "web".
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The existing runtime-mode test covers only the happy path where NIAMOTO_RUNTIME_MODE is exactly "desktop"; it does not cover missing, misspelled, or unsupported values.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a test that sets NIAMOTO_RUNTIME_MODE to an unsupported value and asserts the response mode is "web" with project_switching false.
items.minimumFixScope: Update get_runtime_mode normalization and add one focused test in tests/gui/api/routers/test_health.py.

items.title: Les résumés WKT avec limit=0 perdent le type de géométrie
items.recommendation: Calculer les types de géométrie dans le fallback WKT indépendamment de la page demandée, par exemple pendant le passage de statistiques WKT, ou ajouter une requête/parcours léger qui collecte les types distincts même lorsque limit=0.
items.reproduction: Sur une référence WKT qui utilise le fallback, appeler GET /spatial-map/{reference_name}?limit=0. La réponse contient les compteurs et la bbox, mais geometry_types reste vide et geometry_kind vaut unknown même si les WKT sont des POLYGON ou POINT.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Le fichier tests/cli/test_stats.py importe et exerce niamoto.cli.commands.stats, sans test de contrat pour GET /spatial-map/{reference_name} ni pour limit=0.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Ajouter un test avec une table WKT contenant au moins un POLYGON, appeler le builder ou la route avec limit=0, et vérifier que geometry_types contient POLYGON et que geometry_kind vaut polygon.
items.minimumFixScope: Étendre le fallback WKT pour produire geometry_types/geometry_kind hors de la boucle de pagination et couvrir le cas limit=0.

items.title: Export widget list can return null instead of an array
items.recommendation: Normalize the export widgets value before returning it, for example `widgets = group.get("widgets") or []`, and reject non-list values with a clear 400 if desired.
items.reproduction: Create an export.yml group such as `exports: [{groups: [{group_by: plots, widgets: null}]}]` and call `GET /api/config/export/plots/widgets`; the handler returns None rather than an array.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included tests cover null `widgets_data` for transform widget routes, but they do not exercise `GET /export/{group_by}/widgets` with `widgets: null` in export.yml.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a FastAPI route test that writes an export.yml group with `widgets: null`, calls `/api/config/export/plots/widgets`, and asserts HTTP 200 with `[]`.
items.minimumFixScope: Change `list_export_widgets` to normalize null export widgets to an empty list and add a focused route regression test.

items.title: Import schema omits the documented id_field key
items.recommendation: Post-process the generated JSON Schema for EntitySchema so it advertises id_field as the public config key, or adjust the model schema generation/configuration so validation aliases include the documented id_field input while preserving the schema alias where needed.
items.reproduction: Inspect the response from GET /api/config/import/v2/schema and check $defs.EntitySchema.properties: it contains id but not id_field, while valid import.yml examples use schema.id_field.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included test exercises id_field as valid import config data, but it does not call /api/config/import/v2/schema or assert that the returned EntitySchema properties include id_field.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a route test for GET /api/config/import/v2/schema asserting that $defs.EntitySchema.properties includes id_field and that the schema represents the same entity shape accepted by GenericImportConfig.
items.minimumFixScope: Update get_import_v2_schema or the import config model schema metadata, plus one focused route test.

items.title: Table metadata failures are silently returned as successful zero or empty values
items.recommendation: Do not swallow all exceptions in these helpers. Either let failures propagate to the route's 500 response, or include an explicit per-table error field in the response model so clients can distinguish partial metadata failures from real values.
items.reproduction: Monkeypatch db.get_columns to raise for one listed table, or make SELECT COUNT(*) fail for a table. GET /api/data/tables still returns that table with empty columns or count 0 instead of surfacing an error.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included /tables test covers the happy path with a healthy DuckDB fixture. There is no test for count or column introspection failure, so the silent fallback behavior is not asserted as intentional.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a /tables test where get_table_columns or get_table_count raises and assert the API returns an error response, or an explicit per-table error if partial results are the desired contract.
items.minimumFixScope: Replace the broad exception-to-default fallbacks in get_table_count and get_table_columns with explicit error propagation or a documented partial-result contract.

items.title: Malformed datasets section can produce a 500 response
items.recommendation: Validate that import_config, import_config['entities'], and entities['datasets'] are dictionaries before membership/indexing, mirroring the 400 responses used by update_dataset_config.
items.reproduction: Create config/import.yml with entities.datasets as a YAML list containing the requested dataset name, then call GET /api/config/datasets/<that-name>/config. The route reaches datasets_section[dataset_name] and returns 500.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: tests/common/test_config.py covers Config loading and one transform widget API route, but it does not exercise GET /api/config/datasets/{dataset_name}/config with malformed entities.datasets content.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a TestClient test that patches get_working_directory to a temp project whose import.yml has entities.datasets as a non-dict, then assert GET /api/config/datasets/foo/config returns 400 with a malformed import.yml detail instead of 500.
items.minimumFixScope: src/niamoto/gui/api/routers/config.py:get_dataset_config plus a focused route regression test.

items.title: Reference-level pause success path is not covered
items.recommendation: Add a success-path router test for POST /api/enrichment/pause/taxons that monkeypatches pause_reference_enrichment, captures reference_name and source_id, returns {"status": "paused"}, and asserts reference_name == "taxons" and source_id is None.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: test_pause_default_route_calls_default_service covers /pause, test_pause_reference_route_forwards_selected_source covers /pause/{reference_name}/{source_id}, and test_pause_reference_route_maps_value_errors covers /pause/{reference_name} only when the service raises. None verify the successful reference-level forwarding contract.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add test_pause_reference_route_forwards_reference_name analogous to resume/cancel reference tests, posting to /api/enrichment/pause/taxons and asserting captured == {"reference_name": "taxons", "source_id": None}.
items.minimumFixScope: tests/gui/api/routers/test_enrichment.py

items.title: Upload route has no aggregate request size or file count limit
items.recommendation: Add a maximum file count and aggregate byte budget for the whole request, incrementing the aggregate as each file or ZIP member is read and rejecting once the total exceeds the configured limit. Consider streaming regular files to disk with bounded buffering instead of joining all chunks into one bytes object.
items.reproduction: POST a multipart request with many files whose individual sizes are below MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE_BYTES; each file is accepted and written, so total request size and total disk usage can greatly exceed the configured 100 MB limit.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The current tests only monkeypatch the limit for one regular file and one ZIP archive; they do not send multiple near-limit files or assert total request limits.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a test that monkeypatches MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE_BYTES to a small value, uploads multiple files each below the per-file limit but above the intended aggregate limit together, and asserts the request reports an error without writing all files.
items.minimumFixScope: Update upload_files and _read_uploaded_content or its caller to track aggregate size and file count for the request.

items.title: Source-specific start errors are not covered
items.recommendation: Add a focused test that monkeypatches start_reference_enrichment to raise a selected-source validation error and asserts the route returns the intended HTTP status and detail.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The only test that calls /api/enrichment/start/taxons/endemia asserts a 200 response and forwarded source_id. Error mapping tests target /api/enrichment/start and /api/enrichment/start/taxons instead.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a test posting to /api/enrichment/start/taxons/gbif where the patched start_reference_enrichment raises ValueError("No enrichment source 'gbif' found for reference 'taxons'") and assert status_code == 404 plus the detail message.
items.minimumFixScope: tests/gui/api/routers/test_enrichment.py

items.title: GET /job behavior is untested
items.recommendation: Add focused router tests for GET /api/enrichment/job that monkeypatch get_current_job to return a job payload and to return None, asserting the 200 serialized EnrichmentJob response and the 404 {"detail": "No active job"} response.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Existing tests cover config, preview, start, pause, resume, cancel, stats, results, and entities routes, but there are no assertions for /api/enrichment/job or get_job_status.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: In tests/gui/api/routers/test_enrichment.py, add test_get_job_status_returns_active_default_job and test_get_job_status_returns_404_when_no_active_job using gui_duckdb_client.get("/api/enrichment/job") with get_current_job monkeypatched.
items.minimumFixScope: tests/gui/api/routers/test_enrichment.py

items.title: ready_for_analysis can be true with no usable shape geometries
items.recommendation: For readiness, compute at least a bounded valid geometry count for each shape table, or add a separate valid_shape_count/with_geometry field and require it to be greater than zero before setting ready_for_analysis.
items.reproduction: Create a valid occurrence geometry row and a spatial reference table with a configured geometry column containing only NULL or invalid WKT values. GET /api/stats/geo-coverage will set ready_for_analysis true because has_geometry is based on the column, not valid shape rows.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The existing GUI tests exercise invalid WKT handling for POST /geo-coverage/analyze and /geo-coverage/distribution, but there is no GET /geo-coverage test that asserts quick readiness against invalid or empty shape geometries.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a GET /api/stats/geo-coverage test with valid occurrences and a shape table containing only invalid/null geometry, asserting ready_for_analysis is false or the response exposes zero usable shapes.
items.minimumFixScope: Update get_geo_coverage shape readiness calculation and add a focused GUI API test.

items.title: POST /query lacks route-level coverage for filtering, ordering, and column selection
items.recommendation: Add route-level tests against the DuckDB fixture that combine columns, where, order_by, limit, and offset, plus a negative case for unknown filter/order columns returning 400.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Helper tests prove _build_where_clause can produce SQL fragments, but they do not exercise FastAPI request parsing, query construction, session execution, or response serialization for those fields.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: POST /api/data/query with a fixture table, columns=["id"], where="full_name LIKE '%Araucaria%'", order_by="id DESC", and assert columns, rows, total_count, and page_count; add unknown where/order_by column cases asserting 400.
items.minimumFixScope: Tests only; no production change is required for this gap.

items.title: Representatives query leaves the database engine undisposed
items.recommendation: Use the existing database context-manager pattern for this worker or add a best-effort db.engine.dispose() in the finally block after close_db_session().
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The successful representatives tests assert response shape and query behavior, while the worker-thread test replaces run_in_threadpool with a fake and never executes _get_representatives_sync cleanup or checks engine disposal.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a unit test around _get_representatives_sync with a fake Database whose engine.dispose records calls, patch resolve_reference_table to return None, and assert both close_db_session and engine.dispose run before returning an empty response.
items.minimumFixScope: Update _get_representatives_sync cleanup for the Database it creates and add one targeted lifecycle regression test.

items.title: Preview badge claims fake data even when real items are rendered
items.recommendation: Track whether the mock fallback was used, for example used_mock_items = items_data is None before generating mock items, and only inject the fictitious-data badge in that branch. Alternatively use neutral copy when real data is available.
items.reproduction: Patch _load_preview_index_items to return a real item, call POST /api/site/preview-group-index/plots, and inspect the returned HTML. It contains both the real item and the 'Aperçu avec données fictives' badge.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The real-item test asserts that the real title appears, but it does not assert that the fictitious-data badge is absent or conditional.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Extend the real-items preview test to assert that 'Aperçu avec données fictives' is not present, and add/keep a mock-fallback test that asserts the badge is present when _load_preview_index_items returns None.
items.minimumFixScope: Make preview badge injection conditional in preview_group_index and add focused assertions around the badge text.

items.title: Malformed widgets_data turns configured-widget lookup into 500
items.recommendation: Normalize and validate widgets_data before using it. Treat missing or null as {}, reject non-dict values with HTTP 400, and optionally reject non-string widget IDs before building configured_ids.
items.reproduction:
Set config/transform.yml to:
- group_by: taxons
  sources: []
  widgets_data: []
Then GET /api/templates/taxons/configured returns 500 instead of a controlled 400 or empty configured_ids response.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The existing route tests cover a valid group, a non-list top-level transform.yml, and invalid YAML, but they do not cover a matching group whose widgets_data has an invalid or null shape.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a tests/gui/api/routers/test_templates.py case that writes a matching group with widgets_data: [] and optionally widgets_data: null, then asserts a controlled 400 or empty response according to the chosen contract.
items.minimumFixScope: Update get_configured_widgets in src/niamoto/gui/api/routers/templates.py and add the focused route regression test.

items.title: Les erreurs SQL utilisateur sont exposées comme des 500 brutes
items.recommendation: Attraper les erreurs SQL attendues autour de conn.execute, les journaliser côté serveur, et renvoyer une réponse 400 avec un message court et stable; réserver 500 aux erreurs inattendues.
items.reproduction: Avec une base existante, GET /api/database/query?query=SELECT%20*%20FROM%20non_existing_table passe la validation puis renvoie une HTTPException 500 contenant l'erreur du moteur.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Le test fourni vérifie que la couche Database lève une erreur, mais il ne vérifie pas la traduction HTTP ni la sanitisation dans execute_query.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Ajouter un test de route avec SELECT * FROM non_existing_table et vérifier un statut 400 avec un détail stable, sans message brut du driver.
items.minimumFixScope: Ajuster le bloc except de execute_query et ajouter un test de mapping d'erreur pour ce endpoint.

items.title: Asset route lacks direct success and header coverage
items.recommendation: Add route-level tests that monkeypatch importlib.resources.files to a temporary package root, create assets/css/niamoto.css and assets/js/vendor/example.js, then assert 200 responses, expected content-type values, Access-Control-Allow-Origin for CSS/JS, Cache-Control for vendor JS, and representative 404/400 paths.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included tests only inspect generated preview HTML strings containing asset URLs. They never call client.get('/api/site/assets/...') for a successful asset response or assert the response headers produced by serve_niamoto_assets.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add tests in tests/gui/api/routers/test_site.py for /api/site/assets/css/niamoto.css and /api/site/assets/js/vendor/example.js with a temporary assets tree and mocked importlib.resources.files.
items.minimumFixScope: Tests only; no production code change is required unless the new route-level tests expose a behavior mismatch.

items.title: Route-level traversal behavior is not covered for detect-hierarchy
items.recommendation: Add a TestDetectHierarchy case that posts {"filepath": "../../etc/passwd"} to /api/smart/detect-hierarchy and asserts HTTP 400 with an outside-project error.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The detect-hierarchy tests stop at not-found and unsupported-format errors; the path-outside-project assertion exists for analyze-file, relationships, and auto-configure, but not this route.
items.suggestedRegressionTest:
def test_detect_hierarchy_rejects_path_outside_project(self, test_client: TestClient):
    response = test_client.post("/api/smart/detect-hierarchy", json={"filepath": "../../etc/passwd"})
    assert response.status_code == 400
    assert "outside project" in response.json()["detail"].lower()
items.minimumFixScope: Add one route-level regression test under TestDetectHierarchy; no production code change is required if the current service validation already returns 400.

items.title: POST /cancel default route lacks a regression test
items.recommendation: Add focused tests for /api/enrichment/cancel that verify it calls cancel_default_enrichment, returns its payload, and maps a representative ValueError through _raise_http_error.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Existing cancel tests exercise only reference-scoped routes; none posts to /api/enrichment/cancel or monkeypatches cancel_default_enrichment.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add tests similar to test_pause_default_route_calls_default_service and test_cancel_reference_route_maps_service_errors, but targeting /api/enrichment/cancel and cancel_default_enrichment.
items.minimumFixScope: tests/gui/api/routers/test_enrichment.py

items.title: Export listing follows symlinked files outside the exports tree
items.recommendation: While listing, skip symlinks and only include regular files under the real exports directory. Use lstat/is_symlink before stat, or share a symlink-safe helper with /exports/read, and handle per-entry OSError without exposing raw filesystem errors.
items.reproduction: Create a project with exports/api/linked-secret.json as a symlink to a file outside the project, set get_working_directory() to that project, then GET /api/files/exports/list. The api list includes api/linked-secret.json with metadata from the symlink target.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included symlink tests exercise /exports/read only. The list endpoint has a happy-path path-disclosure regression elsewhere, but no case where a matching export entry is a symlink.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a /exports/list test that creates exports/api/linked-secret.json as a symlink to a file outside the project and asserts the response does not include that path or target metadata.
items.minimumFixScope: Update list_exports enumeration for web/api/dwc entries to reject symlinks before statting or serializing entries.

items.title: La route /active expose un contrat non typé
items.recommendation: Déclarer un contrat explicite, par exemple response_model=ExportStatus | None ou Optional[ExportStatus], et retourner un objet validé comme pour /status/{job_id}.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Le test existant vérifie seulement que le job_id est présent sur un chemin heureux après /execute-cli. Il ne valide ni le schéma OpenAPI, ni la réponse null, ni la sérialisation complète de ExportStatus.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Ajouter un test qui inspecte app.openapi() pour /api/export/active et vérifie que la réponse 200 référence ExportStatus avec null possible, plus un test runtime pour le cas sans job actif.
items.minimumFixScope: Modifier le décorateur ou l’annotation de get_active_export_job et ajouter un test d’API ciblé.

items.title: Internal exception text is returned to API clients
items.recommendation: Return a stable generic 500 detail such as 'Internal error retrieving suggestions' while keeping logger.exception for diagnostics.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The linked transformer_suggestions tests cover success and 404 cases, but they do not force an unexpected backend exception and assert that sensitive exception text is redacted.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Monkeypatch EntityRegistry.get or open_database to raise RuntimeError('/private/path secret') and assert the response is 500 with a generic detail that does not contain the secret text.
items.minimumFixScope: Change only the catch-all HTTPException detail for get_transformer_suggestions and add the redaction regression test.

items.title: SSE contract test accepts YAML that is not JSON
items.recommendation: Parse streamed payloads with json.loads and assert the expected event fields and types after JSON decoding.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The current assertions validate semantic keys after YAML parsing, not JSON syntax compatibility for clients.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Replace yaml.safe_load with json.loads in the SSE stream tests; if there is a stream formatting helper, add a negative check that non-JSON data is not accepted as valid output.
items.minimumFixScope: Update the auto-configure job SSE tests in tests/gui/api/routers/test_smart_config.py.

items.title: Index generator tests write to a shared /tmp path
items.recommendation: Replace self.test_output_dir with a per-test temporary directory, for example by using pytest tmp_path or tempfile.TemporaryDirectory in setUp/tearDown, and ensure generated files stay under that directory.
items.reproduction: Run `rm -rf /tmp/test_output && uv run pytest -q -p no:cacheprovider tests/core/plugins/exporters/test_index_generator.py::TestIndexGeneratorPlugin::test_generate_index_no_data`, then observe `/tmp/test_output/taxon` exists afterward.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The tests mock file writes and assert rendered context, but they do not assert filesystem isolation or cleanup after generate_index.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a test that runs generate_index with a tmp_path output directory and asserts no `/tmp/test_output` path is created or used.
items.minimumFixScope: Update TestIndexGeneratorPlugin setup/output-dir handling and any generate_index tests that rely on the fixed path.

items.title: Full JSON API integration test is a no-op
items.recommendation: Implement the test with a minimal real database and assert generated detail/index/metadata files, or remove/xfail the placeholder so the suite does not overstate coverage.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The test body contains no setup, action, or assertion.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Create a tmp DuckDB database with one group table, run JsonApiExporter.export into tmp_path, and assert the expected detail JSON, index JSON, and metadata file contents.
items.minimumFixScope: Replace TestJsonApiExporterIntegration.test_full_export_with_real_data with a real integration scenario or delete the placeholder.

items.title: GitHub API fallback is not exercised through the public deploy path
items.recommendation: Add a public deploy(config) test with git unavailable and a file count under GITHUB_API_SAFE_FILE_LIMIT, using a fake AsyncClient to verify the API fallback is selected and receives the expected file list/branch/token flow.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The existing private-helper tests bypass deploy(), and the public no-git test intentionally stops at the large-site guard.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Monkeypatch shutil.which to return None, create a small exports directory, fake httpx.AsyncClient, call GitHubDeployer().deploy(config), and assert the API fallback creates blobs, tree, commit, ref update, .nojekyll, and success URL via the public entrypoint.
items.minimumFixScope: Add one focused public GitHub deploy fallback test in tests/core/plugins/deployers/test_github.py.

items.title: I18n fallback tests use identical translations, hiding wrong language selection
items.recommendation: Use distinct values for each language and assert the exact expected fallback according to available_languages order.
items.reproduction: Change the resolver to choose the English entry before the French entry in this scenario; the current test still passes because both values are "Distribution".
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: These specific language-selection tests collapse multiple possible outcomes to the same string, so they do not verify the branch they describe.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Use value={"fr": "Texte FR", "en": "Text EN"} with default_lang="de" and assert resolving "es" returns "Texte FR" when available_languages order is de/fr/en.
items.minimumFixScope: tests/common/test_i18n.py language fallback fixtures and assertions.

items.title: Unique function test masks ordering regressions
items.recommendation: Assert the exact expected sequence for unique, preferably preserving first occurrence order from the input, or explicitly document and test that order is intentionally unspecified.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: This test is the only unique coverage and it discards sequence information before comparing.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: assert self.resolver.resolve("@step1.mixed_list|unique") == [3, None, 1, 2]
items.minimumFixScope: Tighten test_resolve_function_unique in tests/core/plugins/transformers/chains/test_reference_resolver.py.

items.title: Transform-group edge cases are set up but not actually asserted
items.recommendation: Assert the overlapping `taxons` collection remains `source_type == "reference"` in the transform-group test, and assert `list_sources()` as an exact list or at least check both the tuple set and `len(sources)` so duplicates cannot slip through.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Current assertions skip the colliding `taxons` collection in one test and use a set comprehension in the other, which discards duplicate entries and ordering before the assertion runs.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add assertions that `collections["taxons"].source_type == "reference"` when transform config also contains `group_by: taxons`, and that `list_sources()` returns exactly one `("transform_group", "plot_stats")` option.
items.minimumFixScope: Update `tests/core/collections/test_collection_catalog.py` assertions only.

items.title: Profiler tests miss encoded TSV/TXT fallback interaction
items.recommendation: Add a Latin-1 encoded tab-delimited .txt or .tsv fixture with accented headers or values and assert that the profiler returns the correct row count and separate columns.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The current TSV/TXT tests do not exercise non-UTF-8 input, and the current Latin-1 test does not exercise tab-delimited input.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Create occurrence.txt with latin-1 encoding and tab separators, for example headers with espèce/localité, then assert record_count == 3 and len(profile.columns) == 3.
items.minimumFixScope: Add one test case under TestEncodingFallback or TestTsvTxtSupport in tests/core/imports/test_profiler_io.py.

items.title: ECE test does not verify the stated perfect-calibration behavior
items.recommendation: Split this into exact known-value cases: confidence 1.0 with all-correct predictions should assert ECE 0.0, and confidence 0.85 with all-correct predictions should assert approximately 0.15.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The only non-empty ECE assertion is a broad upper bound on a non-perfect-calibration fixture, so it cannot distinguish the documented zero-error case from a merely small error.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add assert _compute_ece(["a", "a"], ["a", "a"], [1.0, 1.0]) == 0.0 and assert _compute_ece(["a", "a"], ["a", "a"], [0.85, 0.85]) == pytest.approx(0.15).
items.minimumFixScope: Update TestECE in tests/core/imports/test_evaluation_harness.py.

items.title: Option-specific render tests only prove a Plotly container was emitted
items.recommendation: For option-specific tests, inspect the generated figure data/layout or mocked Plotly call arguments. Assert sorted x/y sequences, positive/negative colors around the threshold, and one trace per configured map layer with the expected style.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The assertions never inspect traces, serialized figure JSON, or Plotly call arguments for the option being exercised.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Patch render_plotly_figure or Plotly constructors to capture the Figure, then assert the exact trace data/layout affected by each option.
items.minimumFixScope: Strengthen the cited option-specific tests so each verifies the semantic effect of the parameter it configures.

items.title: Logging tests assert presence instead of the structured behavior they set up
items.recommendation: Assert the actual structured outcomes: inspect the console handler formatter for the custom format and inspect caplog.records for error_details containing NiamotoError details and additional_info.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The current assertions stop at handler/message presence and never inspect formatter configuration or the extra fields attached to emitted log records.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add assertions such as checking the console handler formatter format string and caplog.records[-1].error_details for details and additional_info.
items.minimumFixScope: Strengthen the affected tests in tests/common/utils/test_logging_utils.py.

items.title: Direct unittest execution fails because unittest.mock is never imported
items.recommendation: Import patch explicitly with from unittest.mock import patch and use patch(...), or add import unittest.mock before the test references unittest.mock.
items.reproduction: .venv/bin/python tests/common/utils/test_data_access.py -q raises AttributeError: module 'unittest' has no attribute 'mock'.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The linked pytest workflow can pass because pytest or plugins may import unittest.mock before this test runs; the file's __main__ unittest path is not covered by pytest itself.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Either add a smoke check for direct unittest invocation of this file or remove the __main__ runner if direct execution is not supported.
items.minimumFixScope: Update tests/common/utils/test_data_access.py imports and the one patch call.

items.title: Legacy fallback test has no behavioral assertion
items.recommendation: Assert the expected fallback semantics explicitly, such as the exact empty result if legacy matching should be disabled, or the expected legacy widget names/reasons if mappings should be returned.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included test deliberately accepts either empty results or legacy mappings and checks only the container type, so it cannot fail on incorrect fallback content.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Update test_legacy_fallback_no_structure to assert the exact expected suggestions for MockTransformerNoStructure, including widget names and scores/reasons when applicable.
items.minimumFixScope: Strengthen tests/core/plugins/matching/test_pattern_matching.py around the no-output_structure legacy fallback case.

items.title: Custom entity-name tests do not exercise custom entity resolution
items.recommendation: Rename these as plain transform acceptance tests or replace them with a service-level test that exercises source resolution through EntityRegistry.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The helper _resolve_table_name tests cover the helper in isolation, while these transform tests bypass the code path that consumes source and selects/resolves the entity.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Create a TransformerService integration test with params.source="habitat_stats", a registry mock returning a physical table name, and an assertion that the custom source is actually resolved or selected.
items.minimumFixScope: Update the two custom-entity transform tests; add one focused service-level custom-source test if custom entity behavior is the intended contract.

items.title: Missing-axis-column test locks in an opaque conversion error
items.recommendation: Split missing-column validation from numeric conversion: assert a DataTransformError that names non_existent_axis_col, and keep the separate text_axis case for conversion failures.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The existing missing-column test asserts the opposite of the desired diagnostic; the adjacent conversion test already covers the generic conversion path.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a missing-axis-column assertion expecting the configured field name in the error message, for example match="non_existent_axis_col".
items.minimumFixScope: Update the series-by-axis missing-column test and, if needed, add an explicit axis column presence check before numeric conversion.

items.title: sort=False matrix test would pass if the axis is sorted
items.recommendation: Assert exact axis order and fixed aligned values for the sample data instead of normalizing with sorted() and deriving expectations from the result.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The assertion removes ordering from the comparison, and the value assertion is self-referential because it uses actual_axis from the result.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Assert result["elevation"] == [200, 100, 300] and result["series"]["s1"] == [5, 2, 8] for the existing sample data with sort=False.
items.minimumFixScope: Strengthen test_transform_axis_not_numeric_or_sorted only.

items.title: Custom fragmentation size classes are accepted but never exercised
items.recommendation: Assert validated.params.size_classes in the config test and add a transform test where custom size classes change the labels/counts/areas.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The only size_distribution output assertion uses the default buckets, while the custom bucket configuration is validated superficially without behavioral coverage.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Run FragmentationAnalysis with metrics=["patch_count", "size_distribution"] and size_classes=[0, 50, 200, float("inf")], then assert labels, counts, areas, and percentages match those custom buckets.
items.minimumFixScope: Extend test_valid_config_full and add one custom-size_distribution case in test_fragmentation.py.

items.title: BHL credential redaction assertion is not exercised by the fixture
items.recommendation: Add a focused BHL request/redaction test that exercises the real request-building path with requests_mock, uses an API key value unlikely to appear by accident, and asserts neither the key nor parameter name is present in persisted raw/processed enrichment output.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The structured BHL test bypasses _bhl_request_json and supplies sanitized fake raw payloads directly, so it never creates a payload that could reveal whether secrets are stripped.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Mock the BHL endpoint through requests_mock while letting the real BHL helper build query params, then assert the outgoing request includes the API key but api_response_raw and api_response_processed do not contain the key value or credential parameter.
items.minimumFixScope: Replace the vacuous redaction check with one test that exercises the real BHL API request path.

items.title: Sorting assertions can pass when expected rows are absent
items.recommendation: Assert each searched token is present before comparing positions, or parse the rendered table and assert the exact ordered row values.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The same find result is used directly as the ordering predicate, so absence of the earliest expected item is not independently checked.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: For each sorting test, extract the species column from the rendered HTML and assert the exact sequence, such as ["Species A", "Species B", "Species C"].
items.minimumFixScope: Update the affected sorting assertions in tests/core/plugins/widgets/test_raw_data_widget.py and tests/core/plugins/widgets/test_table_view.py.

items.title: Hover-data string conversion is not actually exercised
items.recommendation: Either pass hover_data as a string and assert the intended behavior, or rename the test to cover the list-only contract and add an explicit validation test for string input.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The current test constructs ScatterPlotParams with hover_data=["info"], so it only covers the normal list path.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a test that calls ScatterPlotParams(x_axis="x", y_axis="y", hover_data="info") and asserts either successful rendering after conversion or the expected validation error, depending on the intended API contract.
items.minimumFixScope: Update tests/core/plugins/widgets/test_scatter_plot.py.

items.title: Sunburst deterministic ordering test does not assert ordering
items.recommendation: Patch render_plotly_figure and assert the generated figure trace ids or labels are in the expected sorted order, or normalize Plotly-generated IDs before comparing outputs.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The test stops after basic render-success assertions and leaves the intended same-elements/order assertion as a comment.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Assert fig.data[0].ids equals the expected root/category/leaf sequence for the z_category/a_category fixture.
items.minimumFixScope: Update tests/core/plugins/widgets/test_sunburst_chart.py.

items.title: Disjoint-value relationship test permits other invalid matches
items.recommendation: Tighten the assertion to reject any returned relationship from plot_id in this fixture, or assert relationships is empty if no other candidate is expected.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The current predicate only checks target_field == "plot_id"; it permits plot_id -> plot_name and also passes vacuously when relationships is empty.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Change the final assertion to `assert relationships == []`, or assert `all(relationship["source_field"] != "plot_id" for relationship in relationships)`.
items.minimumFixScope: Update tests/core/utils/test_column_detector_relationships.py to assert the full disjoint-sample expectation.
