items.title: ProgressManager.complete_task is only smoke-tested
items.recommendation: Assert the Rich task state inside the context after complete_task, including completed == total and a description containing the escaped success message and completion marker.
items.reproduction: Replace complete_task with a return after validating the task exists; this test would still pass because it only checks that no exception is raised.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: There are no assertions after complete_task is invoked; the neighboring nonexistent-task tests intentionally verify only graceful no-op behavior.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: After completing a task with total=100, assert the tracked Rich task has completed == 100 and that its description includes the escaped success message.
items.minimumFixScope: Strengthen TestProgressManager.test_complete_task in tests/cli/utils/test_progress.py.

items.title: Import metrics tests omit thousands-separated counts
items.recommendation: Add parser cases with formatted counts such as 1,234 taxons and linked=5,000 so normalization of grouped numbers is part of the contract.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: All current parse_import_result examples use contiguous digits, while comma-separated numbers are only tested on the formatting side.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Assert MetricsCollector.parse_import_result("1,234 taxons imported with 45 families") records taxonomy == 1234, and that occurrence linked/unlinked counts with comma separators are parsed correctly.
items.minimumFixScope: Add comma-separated count cases to tests/cli/utils/test_metrics.py.

items.title: Table markup escaping test does not verify cell contents
items.recommendation: Assert the escaped cell value either from the Table internals or by rendering the table with a recording Console and checking the exported text for the literal escaped content.
items.reproduction: Change print_table to escape table titles and headers but add raw row values; this test would still pass because it never inspects the cell containing [bold]Jane[/bold].
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The current test creates a marked-up cell value but never checks the rendered row or stored cell payload.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Extend test_print_table_escapes_rich_markup to assert the body cell contains escape("[bold]Jane[/bold]") or renders as a literal markup string rather than interpreted bold text.
items.minimumFixScope: Add one assertion to tests/cli/utils/test_console.py for the table body cell.

items.title: Unsupported DirectAttribute import test can pass for the wrong reason
items.recommendation: Either remove/rename the stale unsupported-import expectation or wire the test to the actual code path and assert the specific cause, not just the generic wrapper text.
items.reproduction: Make _get_field_value fail during registry lookup before inspecting imports_config; this test still passes because it only checks the generic wrapped message.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The assertion does not distinguish unsupported connector handling from unrelated registry or database failures.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Mock registry.get and the database path so the test reaches the intended unsupported-source branch, then assert the exception cause or message specifically mentions the unsupported type.
items.minimumFixScope: tests/core/plugins/transformers/extraction/test_direct_attribute.py

items.title: Unsafe formula regression test could execute a shell command
items.recommendation: Patch os.system or the relevant import path to raise/assert-not-called before invoking transform, so the test proves no side effect occurs.
items.reproduction: Replace the derived formula evaluator with unsafe eval; this test input reaches os.system('echo unsafe') unless the test environment blocks it.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The current test only expects ValueError; it does not assert that dangerous call sites were not reached.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Use unittest.mock.patch('os.system', side_effect=AssertionError('os.system executed')) around the unsafe formula case and keep the ValueError assertion.
items.minimumFixScope: tests/core/plugins/transformers/extraction/test_multi_column_extractor.py

items.title: Time-series tests only enforce an upper bound for bounded percentages
items.recommendation: Strengthen these assertions to verify every returned series has exactly 12 values and that all values satisfy 0 <= value <= 100. Consider also asserting the expected labels length.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The current tests assert exact values only for indexes 0 and 1, then use an upper-bound-only predicate over whatever values are present.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add assertions such as `assert len(result["month_data"]["value"]) == 12`, `assert all(0 <= value <= 100 for value in result["month_data"]["value"])`, and the same length/lower-bound checks for each multi-field series.
items.minimumFixScope: Update tests/core/plugins/transformers/distribution/test_time_series_analysis.py assertions for single-field and multi-field outputs.

items.title: Browser-opening test only checks timer scheduling, not the browser side effect
items.recommendation: Invoke the captured timer callback under a patched webbrowser.open and assert the exact URL for the selected host and port.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The Timer is mocked and never runs; the callback is only checked for callability.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: After capturing timer_args[1], patch niamoto.cli.commands.gui.webbrowser.open, call the callback, and assert it receives "http://127.0.0.1:8080" for the default case.
items.minimumFixScope: Update tests/cli/commands/test_gui.py browser-opening coverage.

items.title: GUI reload test does not assert the reload import target
items.recommendation: Assert the first positional argument passed to uvicorn.run is the expected import string when --reload is used.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The current test reads mock_uvicorn.run.call_args[1] only and never inspects call_args[0][0].
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add assert mock_uvicorn.run.call_args[0][0] == "niamoto.gui.api.app:app" to test_gui_reload_option.
items.minimumFixScope: Update tests/cli/commands/test_gui.py reload assertions.

items.title: Product-level benchmarks run in the default pytest suite
items.recommendation: Mark the product-level benchmark file or class with pytest.mark.slow and, where appropriate, pytest.mark.integration, or split only the cheap unit checks into an unmarked file.
items.reproduction: uv run pytest tests/core/imports/test_auto_config_service_benchmark.py -q completed with 8 passed in 31.48s in this workspace.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The tests assert product behavior but do not assert marker/category selection, so pytest collection still treats these benchmark scenarios as default tests.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a lightweight collection check that benchmark files/classes under tests/core/imports carry slow or integration markers unless explicitly allowlisted.
items.minimumFixScope: Add a module-level pytestmark or class-level mark to tests/core/imports/test_auto_config_service_benchmark.py; keep any genuinely fast smoke checks separate if they should remain in the default suite.

items.title: result_gdf is not tested as data passed to a following geospatial step
items.recommendation: Extend the result_gdf test to include a second geospatial transformer and assert that it receives the GeoDataFrame returned under result_gdf.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The existing result_gdf test has no downstream consumer, and the downstream preservation test uses _gdf instead.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Create a two-step chain where the first mocked geospatial transformer returns {"result_gdf": sample_geodataframe} and the second mocked geospatial transformer asserts its first argument is that GeoDataFrame.
items.minimumFixScope: Add or extend one test in tests/core/plugins/transformers/test_transform_chain.py for result_gdf propagation between steps.

items.title: Suggestion preview fetch hides real HTTP status codes
items.recommendation: Handle HTTPError before the generic URLError branch, read its body when available, and return the real e.code just like the shapes preview script does.
items.reproduction: Mock urlopen to raise urllib.error.HTTPError with code 500; fetch returns status 0 instead of 500.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included tests only cover classify_html and do not mock fetch or urllib HTTPError behavior.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Add a fetch unit test that raises HTTPError(code=500, fp=io.BytesIO(b'boom')) and asserts fetch returns (500, 'boom').
items.minimumFixScope: Change scripts/dev/test_preview_suggestions.py::fetch and add a small mocked urllib regression test.

items.title: Frozen template path test does not verify the bundled resource key
items.recommendation: Replace the lambda with a Mock or side effect that records the requested key, then assert it was called with the expected template resource path. Alternatively, return different paths for different keys and assert only the template key produces the system location.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The current test asserts only the returned path, while the monkeypatch makes every possible relative_path return that same path.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Assert get_resource_path is called exactly once with the expected frozen template resource key when ResourcePaths.get_template_paths(project_path=None) is used.
items.minimumFixScope: Update tests/common/test_resource_paths.py.

items.title: Prefix-order test cannot fail when prefixes are checked in the wrong order
items.recommendation: Change the mock so multiple prefixed candidates exist, then assert the resolver returns the first prefix in the supplied order. Optionally also assert db.has_table call order for the no-match path.
items.whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Because only dataset_occurrences returns true, the assertion validates eventual discovery, not ordering among competing existing candidates.
items.suggestedRegressionTest: Set db.has_table to return true for both dataset_occurrences and entity_occurrences, call resolve_existing_table(db, "occurrences", ("dataset_", "entity_", "")), and assert dataset_occurrences is returned.
items.minimumFixScope: Update tests/common/test_table_resolver.py.
